channel.title: q-fin.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: q-fin.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Skewness Dispersion and Stock Market Returns
item.description: arXiv:2604.07870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-sectional dispersion in firm-level realized skewness is significantly and negatively related to future stock market returns. The predictive power of skewness dispersion is robust to in-sample and out-of-sample estimation and is incremental over a broad set of existing predictors, with only a few alternatives retaining independent explanatory ability. Skewness dispersion also delivers substantial economic gains in portfolio allocation. Its forecasting power is concentrated in months with monetary policy announcements, reflecting an information-based mechanism. The empirical evidence suggests that skewness dispersion captures the gradual incorporation of macro news into prices, which is driven by variation in aggregate risk and valuation adjustments.
item.dc:creator: Mykola Babiak, Jozef Barunik, Josef Kurka
